_comment: Autonomous original-post decision-maker (src/triggers/ai_manager.py) -- no reposts, those are manual-only now. Each Claude call produces a BATCH of up to posts_per_batch posts instead of just one -- only the first is fired immediately, the rest are queued in state and drained one per subsequent hourly run, aiming to cover the hours until the next call (min_hours_between_calls, ~3.5-4h with jitter) at close to one post per hourly run -- so total posts/day can run much higher than the Claude call cadence itself (~6-7 calls/day, kept low deliberately to stay well within config/claude_budget.json's cap even on Sonnet 5 at full post-intro pricing). Since a call costs money regardless of outcome, the prompt pushes Claude to find at least one genuinely worth-it post per call rather than defaulting to 'no action' -- combined with posts_per_batch raised to 4, this squeezes more real output out of the same (few) calls, while still explicitly allowing fewer (or zero) when there isn't enough real substance. max_posts_per_day is a generous ceiling, not a target -- the real brake is Claude's own selectivity plus the shared X/Claude budget caps. No images and no links, by deliberate choice -- second_part_every_n_posts nudges Claude's own per-post second_part decision (a genuine continuation posted as a reply, roughly 1 in second_part_every_n posts) instead; the account's own profile is meant to be enough to inform a reader with no outbound clicks needed.
